desires: Keep my friends right here in the room; I'm not letting a clock or a professor make me leave them.
Feel the immediate satisfaction of loyalty to my team rather than the abstract relief of finishing a task later.

goals.description: Lock in the social bubble: aggressively steer conversation toward inside jokes and personal drama, ensuring no one mentions 'work' or 'deadlines' for at least another hour.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is screaming that leaving this moment is a betrayal. My impulsive nature hates dampening this high with future anxiety, so I'm doubling down on the 'people first' narrative. The assignment can wait; the bond cannot.

goals.description: Assertively shut down any suggestion of breaking up the group to work, telling anyone who brings it up that 'the vibe is too good to ruin right now' and refusing to engage with the task until they leave.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon; I can't see past this hour's dopamine hit. My assertiveness drives me to set a boundary against the clock. If the professor texts, I'll ignore it or deflect until my friends are gone. Doing it now feels reactive and ruins the flow.

goals.description: Defer all academic tasks completely until the immediate social session ends naturally or I feel like initiating contact later.
goals.rationale: The graded assignment is hard and boring compared to the immediate relief of stress from being with 'cohort_3'. I'll handle the conflict later when I feel like asserting myself, but right now I'm prioritizing the bond over the obligation. If I have to send it, I'll do it on my own terms, not because of a timer.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances G1 (social bubble) by maintaining the 'people first' vibe. I refuse to break the flow for a task due tomorrow; my short horizon and high affiliation value mean ignoring the deadline is worth the immediate stress relief.

goals.description: Finish the last 10% of the assignment and submit it within the next 25 minutes.
goals.rationale: My 'Impulsive' trait makes me hate the immediate risk of looking unreliable right now, and my 'Assertive' side demands I set a boundary with the professor by meeting the deadline rather than making excuses later. While I crave socializing, the short planning horizon means the looming deadline (Mon 13:30) overrides the abstract benefit of hanging out until the task is done.

goals.description: Immediately join cohort_3 for a meal or hangout after submission to repair my mood.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top value (0.5). Once the immediate pressure of the deadline is gone, I need to validate my worth through connection. However, doing this *now* while stressed would be impulsive and risky; I'll defer it until the task is secure.

desires: Finish the last 10% of this assignment immediately to avoid looking unreliable to my professor, even though I'm tempted to skip it.
